from dataclasses import dataclass

@dataclass
class Triple:
    subject: str
    predicate: str
    object: str

# EVOLVE-BLOCK-START

def predict(triples: list[Triple]) -> str:
    """
    Convert a list of triples into a single fluent sentence.
    Unknown predicates fall back to a generic pattern.
    """
    if not triples:
        return ""

    # Simple predicate‑to‑phrase mapping for more natural wording
    phrase_map = {
        "cityServed": "The city of {o} is served by {s}",
        "elevationAboveTheSeaLevel": "{s} is {o} metres above sea level",
        "elevationAboveTheSeaLevelInFeet": "{s} is {o} feet above sea level",
        "elevationAboveTheSeaLevelInMetres": "{s} is {o} metres above sea level",
        "location": "{s} is located in {o}",
        "operatingOrganisation": "{s} is operated by {o}",
        # measurement‑first style improves BLEU matching
        "runwayLength": "{o} metres is the runway length of {s}",
        "runwayLengthFeet": "{o} feet is the runway length of {s}",
        "runwayName": "{s} has runway {o}",
        "country": "{s} is in {o}",
        "isPartOf": "{s} is part of {o}",
        "1stRunwayLengthFeet": "{o} feet is the length of the first runway at {s}",
        "1stRunwayLengthMetre": "{o} metres is the length of the first runway at {s}",
        "1stRunwayNumber": "{s} has first runway number {o}",
        "1stRunwaySurfaceType": "The first runway at {s} has an {o} surface",
        "2ndRunwaySurfaceType": "{s} has a second runway surface of {o}",
        "3rdRunwayLengthFeet": "{o} feet is the length of the third runway at {s}",
        "3rdRunwaySurfaceType": "{s} has a third runway surface of {o}",
        "4thRunwayLengthFeet": "{o} feet is the length of the fourth runway at {s}",
        "4thRunwaySurfaceType": "{s} has a fourth runway surface of {o}",
        "5thRunwayNumber": "{s} has fifth runway number {o}",
        "5thRunwaySurfaceType": "{s} has a fifth runway surface of {o}",
        "icaoLocationIdentifier": "The ICAO Location Identifier of {s} is {o}",
        "locationIdentifier": "{s} has location identifier {o}",
        "iataLocationIdentifier": "The IATA Location Identifier of {s} is {o}",
        "nativeName": "{s} is also known as {o}",
        "leaderParty": "{s} is governed by the {o} party",
        "capital": "{s} has capital {o}",
        "language": "{s} speaks {o}",
        "leader": "{s} is led by {o}",
        "owner": "{s} is owned by {o}",
        "largestCity": "{s} has largest city {o}",
        "mayor": "{s} has mayor {o}",
        "officialLanguage": "{s} has official language {o}",
        "city": "{s} is a city {o}",
        "jurisdiction": "{s} falls under jurisdiction of {o}",
        "demonym": "{s} residents are called {o}",
        "aircraftHelicopter": "{s} operates helicopter {o}",
        "transportAircraft": "{s} operates transport aircraft {o}",
        "currency": "{s} uses the {o}",
        "headquarter": "{s} is headquartered at {o}",
        "class": "{s} belongs to class {o}",
        "division": "{s} belongs to division {o}",
        "order": "{s} belongs to order {o}",
        "regionServed": "{s} serves the region {o}",
        "leaderTitle": "{s} has leader title {o}",
        "hubAirport": "{s} uses hub airport {o}",
        "aircraftFighter": "{s} flies fighter {o}",
        "attackAircraft": "{s} flies attack aircraft {o}",
        "battle": "{s} participated in {o}",
        "countySeat": "{s} has county seat {o}",
        "chief": "{s} is led by chief {o}",
        "foundedBy": "{s} was founded by {o}",
        "postalCode": "{s} has postal code {o}",
        "areaCode": "{s} has area code {o}",
        "foundingYear": "{s} was founded in {o}",
        "ceremonialCounty": "{s} is in ceremonial county {o}",
    }

    # Helper to strip surrounding quotes from object strings
    def _clean_obj(o: str) -> str:
        if isinstance(o, str) and len(o) >= 2 and ((o[0] == '"' and o[-1] == '"') or (o[0] == "'" and o[-1] == "'")):
            return o[1:-1]
        return o

    clauses = []
    for t in triples:
        tmpl = phrase_map.get(t.predicate)
        obj_clean = _clean_obj(t.object)
        if tmpl:
            clause = tmpl.format(s=t.subject, o=obj_clean)
        else:
            # fallback generic wording
            clause = f"{t.subject} has {t.predicate} {obj_clean}"
        clauses.append(clause)

    # Join clauses into a single fluent sentence.
    # Use commas and an Oxford “and” for the final clause to improve naturalness.
    if not clauses:
        return ""
    if len(clauses) == 1:
        sentence_body = clauses[0]
    else:
        sentence_body = ", ".join(clauses[:-1]) + ", and " + clauses[-1]
    sentence = sentence_body.rstrip() + "."
    # Ensure the first character is capitalised
    return sentence[0].upper() + sentence[1:]

# EVOLVE-BLOCK-END

metadata.changes:
Change 1: '"cityServed": "{s} serves the city of {o}",' to '"cityServed": "The city of {o} is served by {s}",'
Change 2: '"icaoLocationIdentifier": "{s} has ICAO identifier {o}",' to '"icaoLocationIdentifier": "The ICAO Location Identifier of {s} is {o}",'
Change 3: '"iataLocationIdentifier": "{s} has IATA identifier {o}",' to '"iataLocationIdentifier": "The IATA Location Identifier of {s} is {o}",'
Change 4: '"1stRunwaySurfaceType": "{s} has a first runway surface of {o}",' to '"1stRunwaySurfaceType": "The first runway at {s} has an {o} surface",'
